reasoning: Warm orange pixel-art explosion with round burst core and scattered particle debris. On dark bg, the orange pops with good contrast and the silhouette reads clearly as an energetic burst — has genuine pixel-toy charm. On light/cream bg, the pale center core bleeds into the background, weakening the central silhouette; outer scattered pixels also lose contrast and become nearly invisible. At 1x the sprite is quite small and reads generically as 'fire dot' — personality disappears entirely. Charm is present in the warm palette and round bubbly core but the multi-bg failure and 1x loss prevent a pass. Not mushy or edgy, just insufficiently contrast-tuned for bright surfaces.
